architecture_summary: Post-disaster image only; single encoder; no explicit before/after fusion; joint segmentation plus tile-level classification head
result_summary: Highest joint score and highest classification F1 so far; strongest classifier among all completed runs
comparison_note: Peaked before the end and dropped by epoch 50, so early stopping should save compute and preserve the best checkpoint

architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder with before and after features concatenated only at the bottleneck; simpler change model than multiscale variants
result_summary: Most stable top model; strongest balance of segmentation quality and classification quality among the leaders
comparison_note: Peak arrived near the end, so this architecture still looks undertrained enough to justify a longer max-epoch budget with early stopping

architecture_summary: Separate before and after encoders with concat plus absolute-difference fusion across bottleneck and skip scales; richest explicit change representation tried so far
result_summary: Competitive on joint score with strong classification, but no clear win over simpler architectures
comparison_note: Overfit after a mid-training peak, which suggests added architectural complexity may be hurting stability

architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder with concatenation at the bottleneck and decoder skip scales; explicit multiscale change fusion
result_summary: Best segmentation Dice and IoU of all completed runs, but joint score trails the leaders because classification is weaker
comparison_note: Only had a 20-epoch budget, so this is not yet a fair max-epoch comparison against the 50-epoch tuned leaders

architecture_summary: Original shared-encoder Siamese U-Net baseline with before and after features concatenated at the bottleneck and skip levels
result_summary: Best result from the baseline trainer; strong segmentation baseline but classification still lags behind tuned change-family models
comparison_note: Useful baseline reference, but the newer change-family recipes now outperform it on the joint objective

architecture_summary: Shared Siamese encoder with concat fusion only at the bottleneck; early proof-of-concept version of the configurable change model
result_summary: Showed the change-family code path works, but the run was too short and under-tuned to compete with later experiments
comparison_note: Not directly comparable to the tuned 20-epoch and 50-epoch runs because both the training budget and recipe were weaker

architecture_summary: Initial shared-encoder Siamese U-Net baseline with before and after features concatenated at the bottleneck and skip levels
result_summary: Second 5-epoch baseline sanity run; modest classification improvement over the first short baseline
comparison_note: Run selected checkpoints by Dice rather than joint score, so it is included mainly for historical comparison

architecture_summary: Initial shared-encoder Siamese U-Net baseline with before and after features concatenated at the bottleneck and skip levels
result_summary: First short end-to-end baseline sanity run; confirmed the pipeline, logging, and outputs worked
comparison_note: Run selected checkpoints by Dice rather than joint score, so it is included mainly for historical comparison
